articles.title: When AI Can Answer Everything, What Should We Still Learn?
articles.zh: 当 AI 能回答所有问题，我们还需要学习什么？
articles.summary: The value of learning is shifting from remembering answers to forming better questions, judgment and original connections.
articles.body:
## Answers are becoming cheap

AI can explain a formula, summarize a book and suggest a plan in seconds. That does not make learning useless. It changes where the hard and valuable part begins.

答案正在变得廉价，但这并不意味着学习失去价值，而是学习中真正困难和有价值的部分正在移动。

## Judgment is still earned

A fluent answer can still be wrong, shallow or irrelevant. To judge it, I need enough knowledge to notice what is missing and understand consequences.

流畅的答案仍然可能错误、肤浅或答非所问。判断力无法完全外包。

## Learning creates taste

The more examples I study, the better I can recognize clarity, originality and craft. AI can generate options, but choosing what deserves to exist requires taste.

学习也在形成品味：知道什么是清楚、原创和真正做得好的。
articles.reflection: I will use AI to move faster through repetition, but not to skip confusion too early. Confusion is often the doorway to a better question. / 让 AI 减少重复劳动，但不要过早跳过困惑。
articles.nextStep: For the next month, record one question I improved after using AI instead of only recording the answer. / 下个月记录被 AI 帮助改进的问题，而不只记录答案。

articles.title: I Failed My First Product—and Learned Who to Ask First
articles.zh: 我做砸了第一个产品，但学会了先问谁需要
articles.summary: I spent too long making something polished before discovering that the problem was mostly in my imagination.
articles.body:
## The exciting idea

I began with a feature list and a name. That felt like progress, but I had not spoken to the people I hoped would use it.

我从功能清单和名字开始，因为看得见的制作很像进度，但我还没有和真正可能使用它的人聊过。

## The uncomfortable test

When I finally showed the prototype, people were polite. They understood it, but nobody had the problem often enough to change their habits.

当我展示原型时，大家都很礼貌，却没有足够强烈的问题需要改变习惯。
articles.reflection: A prototype should test the riskiest assumption, not demonstrate how much work I have done. / 原型应该验证最危险的假设，而不是证明投入了多少时间。
articles.nextStep: Interview five potential users and test the need before designing the next prototype. / 下次制作原型前，先访谈五位潜在用户并验证需求。

articles.title: Why Some Stories Travel Further Than Others
articles.zh: 为什么有些故事传播得更远？
articles.summary: Shareable stories give people a useful feeling, idea or identity to pass forward—not merely something to watch.
articles.body:
## A story needs a carrier

Content does not travel by itself. A person chooses to carry it into another conversation.

内容不会自己传播，是人把它带进下一场对话。

## Compression without emptiness

Strong digital stories remove everything that does not serve the central change. They are compressed, but not hollow.

好的数字叙事会删除不服务于核心变化的部分。它被压缩，却不空洞。

## Give the audience a role

The most memorable pieces leave a small gap for the viewer—to recognize themselves, disagree or tell someone else.

最容易被记住的内容，会给观众留一个位置。
articles.reflection: A shareable story offers the audience something useful to carry forward, not merely something to watch. / 可传播的故事会给观众一个值得带走的想法或感受。
articles.nextStep: Before publishing, check whether there is one clear change and one detail worth remembering. / 发布前检查是否有清晰变化和一个值得记住的细节。
